mnemonics.title: Green Software Pillars
mnemonics.content: Remember the 3 pillars: ECH - Energy, Carbon, Hardware Efficiency. Think 'Every Carbon Hit costs Energy' to remember E-C-H.

mnemonics.title: Sustainable Design Steps
mnemonics.content: PRO-FIT: Profile energy, Reduce waste, Optimize code, Fault tolerance, Improve hardware, Test carbon. A sustainable app is 'PROFITABLE' for the environment.

mnemonics.title: ICT Carbon Footprint
mnemonics.content: ICT = 2-4% of global emissions, same as Aviation. Remember: '2-4 = More than Flying through the skies, ICT's carbon flies.'

acronyms.usage: The sector responsible for 2-4% of global carbon emissions
Organization providing standards for sustainable software including SCI specification
Standard metric for measuring carbon emissions of software applications
Emissions that contribute to climate change, including CO2 from ICT operations
Primary energy consumer in computing, target for optimization
Cloud model that enables resource sharing and energy efficiency

visualTips.visualization: Picture a pie chart where ICT is 2-4 slices out of 100, equal to the aviation industry's slice - they're side by side as equals
Imagine a three-legged stool: Energy leg, Carbon leg, Hardware leg - all three needed for stability
Think of a graph showing energy use at different times - schedule heavy tasks when the graph is low (renewable energy available)
Picture a lazy person only doing work when absolutely necessary - resources load only when needed
One big server room splitting into many virtual rooms, each running independently - maximizing one physical space

analogies.analogy: Like a fuel-efficient car that goes farther on less fuel, energy-efficient software accomplishes more work using less power
Like extending the life of appliances by using them properly, efficient software extends hardware lifespan reducing e-waste
Like doing laundry when solar panels produce maximum power - timing tasks to use clean energy
Like one apartment building housing many families instead of building many separate houses - sharing resources efficiently
Like the carbon cost of manufacturing a car, not just driving it - hardware has upfront carbon cost from manufacturing
